prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response:
The pre-initiative snapshot is empty — confirming there are **zero** existing docs, no playground, and no variant-documentation convention at all. That's the root problem. Let me now lay out the full picture and deliver the fix. --- ## Diagnosis The pre-initiative documentation snapshot contains **no files whatsoever**. There is no playground page for the Navigation component, no documentation for its existing variants, and no rule requiring that variant documentation be written alongside variant code. The `solid` appearance was added in isolation and the omission was invisible because no process existed to catch it. --- ## Root Cause **AP-072 — Variant-Documentation Decoupling.** Component variants and their playground/documentation entries are authored in completely separate locations with no enforced relationship between them. There is no definition-of-done, no lint rule, no template, and no CI gate that couples "variant exists in code" → "variant is demonstrated in playground". Every new variant silently widens the gap until someone notices manually. --- ## Fix Four concrete deliverables, in dependency order: ### 1. Navigation component — add `solid` to the prop types and styles **`src/components/navigation/Navigation.tsx`** ```tsx // --- before --- export type NavigationAppearance = 'transparent' | 'frosted'; // --- after --- export type NavigationAppearance = 'transparent' | 'frosted' | 'solid'; // Inside the appearance style map: const appearanceStyles: Record = { transparent: 'bg-transparent text-foreground', frosted: 'bg-surface/60 backdrop-blur-md border-b border-border text-foreground', solid: 'bg-surface border-b border-border text-foreground shadow-sm', }; ``` --- ### 2. Playground page — document **all three** appearance variants **`src/app/playground/navigation/page.tsx`** *(create file)* ```tsx import { Navigation } from '@/components/navigation/Navigation'; import { PlaygroundSection } from '@/components/playground/PlaygroundSection'; import { PlaygroundRow } from '@/components/playground/PlaygroundRow'; /** * PLAYGROUND — Navigation * * VARIANT CONTRACT (enforced by ADR-004): * Every value of NavigationAppearance MUST have a corresponding * in this file. When you add a new variant, add * its row here in the same commit. See docs/adrs/004-variant-docs.md. */ export default function NavigationPlayground() { return (

Navigation — Playground

{/* ── Appearance variants ──────────────────────────────────────── */} {/* transparent (default) */}
{/* frosted */}
{/* solid — ✦ added alongside the variant implementation */}
); } ``` --- ### 3. ADR that encodes the rule permanently **`docs/adrs/004-variant-docs.md`** *(create file)* ```markdown # ADR-004 — Variant-Documentation Parity (AP-072) **Status:** Accepted **Date:** 2025-07-14 **Deciders:** Yilan Gao --- ## Context Appearance variants (and any other prop-driven visual variant) are added to component source files. The playground pages that demonstrate those variants live in a separate directory. Because there is no enforced coupling between the two locations, variants routinely ship without playground coverage. Discovery only happens through manual code review or user feedback — both unreliable. ## Decision **Every distinct value of every visual-variant prop (appearance, size, intent, etc.) MUST have a corresponding `` in the component's playground page, committed in the same PR as the variant implementation.** The contract is declared with a comment block at the top of each playground file (see the Navigation playground for the canonical template). ## Enforcement layers | Layer | Mechanism | |-------|-----------| | **Definition of done** | PR checklist item: "Playground row added for every new variant value" | | **ESLint custom rule** | `local/require-playground-row` (see below) — fails CI if a variant value exists in the type union but no `` with that exact string is present in the corresponding playground file | | **PR template** | `.github/pull_request_template.md` includes the checklist item | | **ADR comment block** | Every playground file opens with the VARIANT CONTRACT block linking back to this ADR | ## ESLint rule skeleton Create `eslint-rules/require-playground-row.js`: ```js // eslint-rules/require-playground-row.js // // Reads the NavigationAppearance (and any *Appearance / *Variant / *Size) // union type from the component file. For each string literal member it // asserts that a exists in the matching // playground file. Exits non-zero if any label is missing. // // Wire up in eslint.config.mjs: // import localRules from './eslint-rules/index.js'; // { plugins: { local: localRules }, rules: { 'local/require-playground-row': 'error' } } module.exports = { meta: { type: 'problem', docs: { description: 'Every variant value must have a PlaygroundRow.' }, schema: [], }, create(context) { // Full implementation: traverse TSUnionType nodes whose parent identifier // ends in Appearance | Variant | Size | Intent, collect string literal // values, then verify the corresponding playground page file contains // for each. Report missing labels. return {}; // ← replace with full traversal in implementation ticket ENG-041 }, }; ``` ## Consequences * **Positive:** Playground coverage is guaranteed by CI, not memory. * **Positive:** New contributors get an explicit checklist item and a concrete example. * **Negative:** Small extra work per variant (~5 lines of JSX). Accepted. * **Risk:** ESLint rule is a skeleton until ENG-041 is completed; the PR checklist and ADR comment block are the enforcement backstop until then. ## Alternatives considered * Storybook — too heavy for a single-author portfolio; this project uses a custom playground instead. * Snapshot tests only — do not guarantee the variant is actually rendered in the playground, only that existing renders don't regress. ``` --- ### 4. PR template so the rule survives contributor turnover **`.github/pull_request_template.md`** *(create or append)* ```markdown ## Checklist ### Component changes - [ ] TypeScript types updated and exported - [ ] Unit / integration tests updated or added - [ ] **If a visual-variant prop value was added or removed:** - [ ] A `` was added/removed in `src/app/playground//page.tsx` **in this PR** (see [ADR-004](../docs/adrs/004-variant-docs.md)) ### General - [ ] `pnpm build` passes locally - [ ] No new ESLint errors (`pnpm lint`) - [ ] Accessibility: keyboard navigation and screen-reader label verified ``` --- ## Principle **Every variant value is a public API surface.** The playground is its living specification. Treat the playground row as part of the component implementation, not as an optional follow-up doc task. The moment variant code and playground code live in separate commits, you've already accepted that they will drift. Encode that expectation in three independently-failing places — the ADR comment block (human), the PR checklist (process), and the ESLint rule (machine) — so that no single layer of forgetfulness can produce an undocumented variant again. This directly counters **AP-072 (Variant-Documentation Decoupling)**.
